name: MLA 9th Edition

general_format.font: Legible font e.g. Times New Roman — regular and italics must contrast clearly
general_format.line_spacing: double-spaced throughout — entire paper including Works Cited
general_format.paragraph_indent: 0.5 inch for first line of every paragraph (use Tab key)
general_format.spaces_after_punctuation: one space after periods and other punctuation marks

page_numbers.placement: upper right-hand corner, 0.5 inch from top, flush right
page_numbers.note: Instructor may ask to omit number on first page — always follow instructor guidelines

general_format.italics_usage: Use italics for titles of longer/container works; use only when absolutely necessary for emphasis

title_page.note: Do NOT make a title page unless specifically requested or it is a group project
title_page.group_project: List all contributor names each on its own line in the header, followed by remaining MLA header info

first_page_header.placement: upper left-hand corner of first page
elements_in_order: Date (Day Month Year format, e.g. 15 March 2024)

paper_title.format: plain — do NOT underline, italicize, bold, or put in quotation marks
paper_title.referring_to_other_works: Use quotation marks and/or italics for other works referenced in title
paper_title.spacing: double space between title and first line of text

headings.general_note: MLA has no strictly prescribed heading system. Consistency is the key requirement.

essays.recommended_format: Arabic numeral + period + space + section name
essays.example: 1. Early Writings  2. The London Years  3. Final Years

books.note: No prescribed system. Headings must be grammatically consistent at each level.
books.rule: If headings are short phrases, all headings at that level must be short phrases (not full sentences)

headings.key_rule: Whatever system you choose, it must be consistent throughout the entire document

in_text_citations.description: Author's last name and page number — no comma between them

basic_formats.signal_phrase: Author named in sentence → page number only in parentheses: Wordsworth stated... (263).
basic_formats.parenthetical: Author NOT named → last name + page number: (Wordsworth 263).
basic_formats.no_page_number: Use first item from Works Cited entry (title, website name, etc.)

three_or_more_authors.note: et al. has a period after 'al' — never a period after 'et'

by_author_count.same_last_name: Include first initial to distinguish: (R. Miller 12) vs (A. Miller 46)
by_author_count.corporate_author: Use corporation name + page number; abbreviate where appropriate
by_author_count.no_known_author: Use shortened title — italicize if book/website, quote marks if article
by_author_count.same_author_multiple_works: Add shortened title: (Murray, Write to Learn 6) or (Murray, A Writer Teaches Writing 3)

special_cases.classic_literary_works: Page number + semicolon + location abbreviation: (Marx and Engels 79; ch. 1)
special_cases.bible: First citation: italicized version + book + chapter.verse: (New Jerusalem Bible, Ezek. 1.5-10). Subsequent: book + chapter.verse only
special_cases.electronic_no_page: Use first item in Works Cited entry; no browser-based paragraph numbers
special_cases.plays_scripts: Each speaker name in ALL CAPS, indented 0.5 inch, followed by period then dialogue

in_text_citations.when_not_needed: Common knowledge, familiar proverbs, well-known quotations

short_quotations.definition: 4 typed lines or fewer of prose OR 3 lines of verse
short_quotations.format: Enclose in double quotation marks, inline in text
short_quotations.citation_placement: Parenthetical citation AFTER closing quotation mark, BEFORE period
short_quotations.punctuation: Periods and commas go AFTER parenthetical. Question marks/exclamations go inside quotes if part of quote, after parenthetical if part of your text

long_quotations.definition: More than 4 typed lines of prose OR more than 3 lines of verse
long_quotations.format: Block quotation — start on new line, indent entire quote 0.5 inch from left margin
long_quotations.quotation_marks: Do NOT use quotation marks for block quotes
long_quotations.citation: Parenthetical citation comes AFTER the closing punctuation of the block quote

verse_quotations.short: 3 lines or fewer — inline, use slash (/) to indicate line breaks with space on each side
verse_quotations.long: More than 3 lines — block format, preserve original line breaks

quotations.omissions: Use ellipsis (...) with spaces to indicate omitted words
quotations.additions: Use square brackets [like this] for words added to a quote for clarity

works_cited.label: Works Cited
works_cited.label_format: centered, plain (do NOT italicize, underline, bold, or put in quotes)
works_cited.placement: separate page at end of paper
works_cited.spacing: double-spaced throughout, no extra spaces between entries
works_cited.hanging_indent: 0.5 inch — first line flush left, all subsequent lines indented
works_cited.alphabetization: by first item in entry (usually author last name); ignore A, An, The
works_cited.punctuation_between_elements: Each element followed by corresponding punctuation — generally commas and periods only

author_rules.format: Last name, First name Middle initial.
author_rules.suffixes: Include Jr., II etc.: King, Martin Luther, Jr.
author_rules.two_authors: First author: Last, First. Second author: First Last (normal order)
author_rules.three_or_more: First author Last, First, et al.
author_rules.same_author_multiple_works: Use three hyphens (---) in place of name for second and subsequent entries, alphabetized by title
author_rules.pseudonyms: Cite better-known name; less-known real name in square brackets: Carroll, Lewis [Charles Dodgson]
author_rules.no_known_author: Alphabetize by title; use shortened title in parenthetical

title_rules.longer_container_works: Italicize — books, websites, journals, magazines, newspapers, TV series, films
title_rules.shorter_works_within_containers: Quotation marks — articles, chapters, episodes, poems, short stories
title_rules.capitalization: Title Case — capitalize all principal words; do NOT capitalize articles (the, an, a), prepositions, or conjunctions unless first word

location_rules.print_page_numbers: p. for single page, pp. for range; omit repeated leading digits: pp. 225-50
location_rules.url: Delete 'http://' prefix; include access date for changeable web content
location_rules.access_date_format: Accessed Day Month Year (e.g., Accessed 4 May 2009)
location_rules.online_database: Include database name in italics; no subscription info needed

book_one_author.template: Last, First. Title of Book. Publisher, Year.
book_one_author.example: Wordsworth, William. Lyrical Ballads. Oxford UP, 1967.

book_two_authors.template: Last, First, and First Last. Title of Book. Publisher, Year.
book_two_authors.example: Gillespie, Paula, and Neal Lerner. The Allyn and Bacon Guide to Peer Tutoring. Allyn and Bacon, 2000.

book_three_or_more_authors.template: Last, First, et al. Title of Book. Publisher, Year.
book_three_or_more_authors.example: Wysocki, Anne Frances, et al. Writing New Media. Utah State UP, 2004.

edited_book.template: Last, First, editor. Title of Book. Publisher, Year.

chapter_in_edited_book.template: Author Last, First. "Title of Chapter." Title of Book, edited by First Last, Publisher, Year, pp. XX-XX.

journal_article_print.template: Last, First. "Title of Article." Journal Name, vol. #, no. #, Month Year, pp. XX-XX.
journal_article_print.example: Best, David, and Sharon Marcus. "Surface Reading: An Introduction." Representations, vol. 108, no. 1, Fall 2009, pp. 1-21.

journal_article_online.template: Last, First. "Title of Article." Journal Name, vol. #, no. #, Month Year, pp. XX-XX. Database Name, doi:XXXXX.
journal_article_online.note: Include DOI if available; otherwise URL

newspaper_article.template: Last, First. "Title of Article." Newspaper Name, Day Month Year, p./pp. XX.

website_page.template: Last, First. "Title of Page." Website Name, Day Month Year, URL. Accessed Day Month Year.
website_page.no_author: "Title of Page." Website Name, Day Month Year, URL. Accessed Day Month Year.

film.template: Title of Film. Directed by First Last, performances by First Last, Studio, Year.

tv_episode.template: "Episode Title." Series Title, created by First Last, season #, episode #, Network, Day Month Year.

translated_book.template: Author Last, First. Title. Translated by First Last, Publisher, Year.

dissertation.template: Last, First. Title of Dissertation. Year, University Name, PhD dissertation.

wikipedia.template: "Article Title." Wikipedia, Wikimedia Foundation, Day Month Year, URL. Accessed Day Month Year.

podcast.template: "Episode Title." Podcast Name, hosted by First Last, Season #, Episode #, Publisher, Day Month Year, URL.

endnotes_and_footnotes.indicator_format: Superscript Arabic numeral in text, after punctuation (except before dash)
endnotes_and_footnotes.symbol_rule: Do NOT use asterisks, angle brackets, or other symbols

types.bibliographic_notes: References to other publications readers may consult
types.content_notes: Brief supplementary information too digressive for main text

endnotes_and_footnotes.general_guidance: MLA discourages extensive use — keep notes minimal

tables.in_text_reference: Lowercase: 'see table 2' (do not capitalize 'table' in running text)
tables.label: Table + Arabic numeral (no punctuation after label+number)
tables.caption: next line below label, title case
tables.note_form_format: First Last format for names; commas replace periods; publication info in parentheses

figures.alignment: no prescribed alignment — embed as fits
figures.label: Fig. + Arabic numeral + period (not bold or italic)
figures.caption: same line as label, title and/or source in note form
figures.if_full_citation_in_caption: Do not need to repeat in Works Cited

source_note_form_differences: Author names in First Last format (not Last, First)

lists.simple_lists: Run into text with commas, or use a colon to introduce

vertical_lists.unnumbered: Use when order does not matter
vertical_lists.numbered: Use when order matters or items are steps
vertical_lists.capitalization: Capitalize first word of each item
vertical_lists.punctuation: End with period if items are complete sentences; no punctuation if short phrases

abbreviations.general_rule: Define abbreviation on first use then use abbreviation consistently

and others (period after al, never after et)

inclusive_language.note: New chapter added in MLA 9th edition
guidelines: Be conscious of gender-specific terms and stereotyped language
Use pronouns carefully and respect authors' stated pronouns
Consider the effect on various audiences
Capitalize group names when members capitalize them (e.g., Black, Indigenous)

stylistics.spelling_consistency: Various spellings acceptable but must be consistent throughout, except in direct quotations

punctuation_guidance.dashes: Em dash for interruption or emphasis
punctuation_guidance.slashes: Used in inline verse quotations to indicate line breaks (with spaces on each side)

capitalization_special: Capitalize names of persons, organizations, titles of works
Follow MLA guidelines for government and organization names
Capitalize group identifiers when members capitalize them

key_changes_from_mla8: Reintroduction of research paper formatting guidance (was absent in MLA 8)
Expanded guidance on tables, figures, and list formatting
More examples for each core element in Works Cited
New section: 'When Author and Title are not Enough' for disambiguation
Guidance on citing pseudonyms and stage names
Apps and databases: cite only when they are containers of the work
Expanded grammar mechanics: spelling, punctuation, capitalization, italics
Notes section: when and how to use notes in text
key_differences_from_apa7: MLA uses author-PAGE (not author-year) for in-text citations
No abstract required or standard in MLA
No title page for individual papers (only group projects)
Works Cited vs References — all cited works only, not a full bibliography
Title Case for Works Cited titles of containers; sentence case NOT used
No DOI label format requirement — DOI or URL as final location element
Headings are flexible, not prescribed levels
Primarily used in humanities, not social sciences
